name: Steel Browser Automation API
what_it_does: Open-source cloud browser infrastructure for AI agents, providing managed Playwright and CDP-compatible browser sessions with a session management API that can be self-hosted or used via Steel Cloud.
use_cases: AI agent web automation requiring a real browser without building browser infrastructure
Self-hosted browser sessions for compliance or cost-control scenarios
Playwright or Puppeteer script execution in cloud-managed browser sessions
Multi-step agent workflows requiring persistent browser state across actions
Teams evaluating Browserbase alternatives that want open-source transparency
not_for: Simple HTTP data fetching where JavaScript rendering is not needed
High-volume static HTML scraping where a lightweight HTTP client is more efficient
Production workloads requiring guaranteed enterprise SLAs without self-hosting expertise
best_when: An agent needs managed cloud browser sessions and you want open-source flexibility — either to self-host for cost control or to evaluate the codebase before committing to a managed service.
avoid_when: You need a battle-tested managed service with enterprise support and SLAs, or your team lacks infrastructure experience to run self-hosted deployments reliably.

auth.notes: API key passed as a Bearer token for session management. Self-hosted deployments can configure their own auth layer. No fine-grained scope controls.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Self-hosted: free (Apache 2.0 license); Steel Cloud free tier available with limited included minutes
pricing.paid_tiers: Steel Cloud paid tiers priced by browser minutes; see steel.dev for current pricing
pricing.notes: Self-hosting is fully free for any usage. Steel Cloud is a commercial managed service. Pricing is similar to Browserbase but the open-source option differentiates Steel for cost-sensitive use cases.

performance.rate_limits: Self-hosted: limited only by server resources; Steel Cloud limits depend on plan

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Documentation and error handling are improving but thinner than Browserbase's. Self-hosted deployments rely on Docker/Kubernetes logs for debugging. REST API errors are standard HTTP JSON.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Session creation is not idempotent; each call creates a new session. Browser actions are stateful within a session. Session IDs should be tracked by agents to avoid duplicate sessions.
known_agent_gotchas: As an early-stage open-source project, APIs may change between versions without deprecation periods
Self-hosted deployments require Docker and infrastructure expertise; not a one-click solution
Documentation is thinner than Browserbase; community Discord is the primary support channel
Anti-bot detection (Cloudflare, reCAPTCHA) mitigation is less mature than commercial alternatives
Session lifecycle management must be handled by the agent; leaked sessions consume server resources on self-hosted deployments
Steel Cloud availability and SLAs are not as well-established as competitors with longer track records

security_score_components.security_notes: Open-source codebase is publicly auditable, which is a significant transparency advantage. Self-hosted deployments give full control over data and network. No formal security certifications documented. API key auth only with no scope restrictions.
